After carefully reviewing the retrieved evidence, I conclude that a dedicated pelvic CT scan is typically included within a comprehensive abdominopelvic CT scan for staging renal cell carcinoma. While isolated pelvic imaging was previously questioned, current guidelines explicitly recommend a CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis as a single entity for accurate staging — encompassing assessment of renal veins, adjacent organs, and potential local spread. Therefore, although not a separate or optional component, pelvic evaluation is inherently covered in standard staging protocols, rendering a standalone pelvic CT unnecessary.
